name: Chakra UI principles
description: Core Chakra principles: style props, simplicity, composition, accessibility, dark mode, and prop naming conventions.

metadata.intent: Apply Chakra UI’s core principles to keep components consistent and maintainable

must_use: Style props (override/extend) before reaching for custom styled wrappers
Compose new components from Box when appropriate
must_not: Overcomplicate component APIs with excessive props
conventions: Boolean props use auxiliary verbs: is/has/should (e.g., isDisabled, isLoading).

steps.description: Style props: prefer prop-based styling overrides; reduce reliance on `css` prop or `styled()` for simple variants. Compose from `Box` for new primitives.
Simplicity: keep APIs intuitive and CSS-like; document real-world usage examples rather than theoretical flexibility.
Composition: break components into smaller parts; minimize props; compose together for extensibility.
Accessibility: keyboard navigation, focus management, color contrast, ARIA attributes. Keep a11y top-of-mind; run `pi validate` for common issues.
Dark mode: ensure components work in both modes; use `useColorMode`/theme tokens and test contrast and focus styles in both palettes.
Naming props: boolean props start with is/has/should/does; names should indicate behavior clearly (avoid ambiguous names).
